what_it_does: End-to-end acceptance test for the I6 annotation-at-an-address feature: it exercises Suite.annotate and Suite.annotations_at to attach comments keyed by ui:// addresses, retrieve them by the same address, reject malformed addresses and empty text, verify persistence across Suite reload, and confirm annotations stay separate from chat history and the surfaced inbox.
observations: Sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN for inbox-hygiene parity with sibling suites.
Creates a temporary store, discovers nodes, and instantiates a Suite.
Tests attach/retrieve round-trip for ui:// addresses including a /@state suffix.
Verifies comments at the same address append into a thread, not overwrite.
Verifies comments at different addresses do not leak.
Expects exceptions for malformed addresses, non-ui:// schemes, bare ui://, and empty/whitespace text.
Checks rejected writes left nothing in the store via suite.store.annotations_for.
Checks chat_history and list_surfaced remain untouched, and annotations.jsonl is created.
Reloads a second Suite over the same root to prove on-disk persistence.
Uses a global PASS counter and simple check/expect_raises helpers.
standouts: Address is the primary key for annotation threads.
Malformed address rejection is enforced on both write and read (fail loud).
Annotations live in their own annotations.jsonl file, separate from chat.jsonl and inbox.
Persistence is proven by constructing a second Suite over the same store root.
conventions: Follows AGENTS.md rule 4 by failing loud on empty/whitespace text.
Uses a global mutable PASS counter with assert-based helpers.
Uses tempfile + shutil.rmtree for isolated test storage.
References implementation-level store method annotations_for to verify rejection cleanup.
concerns: Relies on suite.store.annotations_for, an internal store API, to assert rejection cleanup.
Global PASS counter is mutated by helpers, making test flow order-sensitive.
Hardcodes NODES path relative to ROOT.
Does not assert the exact exception type for malformed inputs.
notes: A Suite-level acceptance test that treats the annotation branch as an address-keyed, append-only, persisted comment thread isolated from chat and inbox.
questions: What exactly defines the S0 grammar for ui:// addresses?
What are the I2/I7 lanes and how do they differ from I6?
What is the expected shape and timezone of the ts field?
Should annotations_for be a public store method or an implementation detail?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it proves attach/retrieve-by-address, rejection, persistence, separation, and fail-loud empty text end-to-end at the Suite level.
intention_signals: The file is trying to lock down the I6 annotation branch behavior so that /api/annotate and /api/annotations can safely wrap Suite.annotate and Suite.annotations_at.
novelty: It introduces a separate annotation content branch keyed by ui:// addresses rather than annotating existing surfaced items by id.
contribution: Provides an executable acceptance contract for address-keyed annotations, including round-trip behavior, grammar gating, persistence, isolation from chat/inbox, and empty-text rejection.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test for the I6 annotation-at-an-address feature using Suite.annotate and Suite.annotations_at over a temporary FsStore.
intention_for_embedding: To validate and document the end-to-end behavior of the address-keyed annotation branch before thin API routes wrap it.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global PASS counter when a condition is true.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) asserts the supplied condition, prints an 'ok' line with the label, and increments the module-level PASS counter; on failure it raises with a FAIL message.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that verifies a callable raises an exception.
symbols.description: expect_raises(label, fn) calls fn inside a try/except; if an exception is raised it increments PASS and prints 'ok', otherwise it fails with a 'did not raise' message.
